segments.text: Why does Cadbury Silk taste so much
creamier than the regular dairy milk?
Even though they basically use the same
ingredients, the secret is not in the
recipe, it's in the geometry. See,
standard chocolate is designed as flat
angular bricks. It's efficient, but it
has sharp edges. Can we solve this? In
Silk, they deleted those edges
completely. They redesigned it with
these curved bubbly mounds. But why does
that shape matter? Well, rounded shapes
do melt slightly faster and more evenly
on the tongue, giving that sense of
smoothness. while sharp corners actually
take longer and creating a texture that
feels harder on tongue. So, does the
shape actually change the flavor? The
2013 Cadbury study proved this. Same
recipe, different shape, and consumers
perceived it as creamier. For more
product design secrets, subscribe.
